name: aksarallm-4b-dense

estimated_params_breakdown.comment: Tied embeddings; per-layer attn (Q+O = 2*d*n_heads*head_dim = 18,874,368; K+V = 2*d*n_kv*head_dim = 3,145,728) = 22,020,096; per-layer SwiGLU (3 * d * n_inner = 113,246,208); per-layer norms (2 * d = 6,144); per-layer total = 135,272,448. 32 layers = 4,328,718,336. Tied embedding (V * d = 402,653,184). Final RMSNorm = 3,072. Grand total = 4,731,374,592 (~4.73B). Sized to fit a single v6e-8 (32 GB HBM/chip) using FSDP=8 OR even unsharded if EasyDeL's optimiser-state sharding does not engage — bf16 params (9.46 GB) + factored Adafactor state (~6 GB) + transient gradients (9.46 GB) sums to ~25 GB per chip, comfortably below the 31.25 GB usable budget.

token_plan.comment: Available corpus is 84.64 B BPE tokens after dedup+tokenize. That is ~17.9x params, well above 'minimum viable' (2x) and a healthy ratio for a first-cut Indonesian LLM.

training_plan_v6e8.comment: 1.29M steps * 65,536 tok/step = 84.5 B tokens (~1 epoch over the dedup+tokenized corpus). Picked deliberately as the largest dense LLaMA-style model that fits a single v6e-8 with the current EasyDeL trainer (where nnx.Optimizer does not propagate parameter shardings to the optimiser state). For a first-cut run with usable wall time, set max_steps=200_000 (~13 B tokens, multiple weeks).

notes: AksaraLLM-4B is the production-grade target for v6e-8.
If you ever get access to v6e-32+ or v5p-64, use ``configs/aksara_7b_dense.json`` (or the original ``aksara_20b_dense.json``) instead — both depend on a sharded optimiser state to fit.
Tokenizer: Ezekiel999/aksara-tokenizer-20b (vocab=131072), identical to the 20B/7B configs.
